title: Vatica scortechinii
intro: Vatica scortechinii, also known as the Peninsular Dipterocarp, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The bioactive constituents of Vatica scortechinii include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vatica scortechinii is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Vatica scortechinii is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is native to Malaysia and belongs to the genus Vatica, which consists of approximately 90 species. The genus Vatica is further classified within the subfamily Dipterocarpoideae. The family Dipterocarpaceae is a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical forests of Asia. Vatica scortechinii is an important species in its native habitat, providing timber and habitat for various wildlife.

The bark and leaves of the tree have been employed to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
